marketState: neutral, cautiously bullish tilt — hot summer demand and extreme short COT positioning offset by a large storage surplus
summary: Price sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923 now), so there is no clean mispricing edge today. The most striking signal is weather: the 8-16 day demand outlook is +29.1% vs the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, pointing to strong upcoming cooling demand, while 1-7D demand is also above normal (+11%) but not extreme. Seasonality is a headwind, though a weak one — price is running 7.7pp below its typical seasonal path and the probability of higher prices in 3-4 weeks is only 38%, with low reliability (59pp spread) this week. Storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast), which caps the upside case even with hot weather. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of 5 years, an extreme positioning skew that raises the odds of a short-covering squeeze if demand data keeps surprising hot.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +29.1% vs 5y and above the max historical range, and managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of 5 years — both point to upside risk skew as fair value drifts higher (plus21D fair $3.080, band up to $3.580). Storage's +180 Bcf surplus and bearish build surprise are the main offsetting factor, keeping conviction medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D demand forecast cools back toward normal, storage surprises continue running bearish/larger, or price closes below $2.70 confirming a break of the fair-value support zone.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is essentially at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923, spread -$0.012, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, and technicals conflict across timeframes — 15m/1h lean bullish (RSI 52-58, EMA 50>200 on 15m) while 1d/1w remain in a bearish trend (EMA 50<200, RSI ~37, MACD negative). 1-7D weather demand is elevated (+11% vs 5y) but within normal range, not extreme enough to force a near-term move.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a 1d trend flip (EMA 50>200 cross and MACD histogram turning positive) confirming momentum aligns with the bullish weather/COT case, or a pullback toward $2.75-$2.80 for a lower-risk long entry.

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +29.1% vs 5y and above the max historical range — bullish cooling demand
Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y — extreme positioning raises squeeze risk
Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast) — caps upside
Price essentially at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923) — no near-term mispricing edge
Seasonality below median (-7.7pp) with low reliability (59pp spread) — weak, mixed longer-term bias
risks: Storage surplus could keep pressuring price lower if weather demand moderates
Daily/weekly technicals remain in a bearish trend (death cross, negative MACD), which could invalidate the long thesis
